refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall maintain an operational availability (Ao) of ≥ 0.97, calculated as Uptime / (Uptime + Downtime), over a rolling 30-day evaluation period.
refined_fr.rationale: The original requirement was a high-level KPI; refinement adds a specific metric and evaluation window to make it verifiable and actionable for engineering.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Automated analysis of system logs and heartbeat signals to calculate the ratio of 'Ready for Task' state versus 'Maintenance/Failure' state.
sub_frs: Minimize Mean Time To Repair (MTTR) via onboard diagnostics.
Maximize Mean Time Between Failures (MTBF) through component derating.
Maintain continuous connectivity for remote health monitoring.

candidate_dps: Predictive Maintenance and Health Management (PHM) System
selected_dp.selected_dp: Predictive Maintenance and Health Management (PHM) System

coupling_check.notes: The PHM system must interact with all subsystems but remains logically decoupled from the primary control laws.

assumptions_risks: Assumes sensor data is sufficient to predict 80% of critical failure modes.
Risk: False positives may trigger unnecessary downtime, lowering availability.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Ensure the DP includes a 'Limp Home' mode logic to maintain partial availability during sensor failure.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The top-level system wrapper that orchestrates vehicle health data and remote service coordination.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Streaming health metrics and diagnostic codes to the site management server.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Bi-directional communication for remote diagnostics and software updates.

module_containment_scores.notes: Highest level module; contains the logic for cross-subsystem availability tracking.
Contains physical failure points but not the global availability logic.
Only impacts availability if the remote link is the primary failure mode.
Safety triggers downtime but does not manage the availability KPI.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The NGHE Solution module is the only boundary capable of aggregating data from both the Vehicle and the Network Link to satisfy the 0.97 KPI.
containment_risks: Logic diffusion if individual vehicle components implement their own non-standardized health reporting.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Standardize the diagnostic API across all sub-modules to ensure NGHE Solution can aggregate data.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The PHM system directly addresses the availability target by reducing unplanned downtime.

diffusion_risks: High risk of logic leaking into the 'Vehicle' module if sensors are not abstracted.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The NGHE Solution module is the appropriate container for the 'System of Systems' availability logic.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: High; telemetry data provides a clear audit trail for uptime.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear; the NGHE Solution module owns the KPI.

recommended_revisions: Define specific data schemas for the Telemetry interface to support PHM.

decision.justification: The high-level availability requirement is well-defined and mapped to a PHM strategy, but requires further decomposition into specific subsystem reliability and diagnostic requirements.
next_level_sub_frs: Monitor hydraulic pressure trends for leak prediction.
Detect network latency degradation before link loss.
Log all state transitions to calculate MTTR.

assumptions: Customer provides the network infrastructure for the Network Link.
open_risks: High safety criticality may require redundant PHM sensors, increasing cost.
evidence_needed: Historical reliability data for the base vehicle platform.
